sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: State that this procedure defines how {company_legal_name} identifies, investigates, implements, and verifies corrective and preventive actions per ISO 13485:2016 §8.5.2/§8.5.3 and 21 CFR 820.100. It governs records on form {ref:CA-002}.

sections.title: 2. Scope
sections.content: Applies to all quality issues at {company_legal_name} including nonconformities ({ref:CA-007}), complaints ({ref:CA-003}), audit findings ({ref:AU-006}), supplier issues ({ref:SC-006}), and post-market signals ({ref:RA-005}).

sections.title: 3. CAPA Process Steps
clauses: 3.2 Risk-based triage and prioritisation (low / medium / high)
3.4 Root cause analysis (5 Whys, fishbone, fault tree)
3.5 Action plan: corrections, corrective actions, preventive actions
3.7 Effectiveness verification per {ref:CA-008} (define metric, sample size, period)
sections.section_prompt: For each step, describe the responsible role, target timeline (e.g. RCA within 10 working days for high), and required record. Reference linkage to risk file {ref:RD-007} when CAPA changes risk acceptability.

sections.title: 4. CAPA Triggers Matrix
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 8–10 CAPA triggers: complaint trend, repeat nonconformance, supplier SCAR, internal audit major, external audit, recall/FSCA, adverse event, software defect critical, validation deviation, management review escalation. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 5. Roles & Responsibilities
sections.section_prompt: Describe responsibilities of CAPA Owner, QA Manager ({qa_manager_name}), Process Owner, and Management. Be specific about approval authority by CAPA priority level.

sections.title: 6. Records & Metrics
sections.section_prompt: List records produced (CAPA form {ref:CA-002}, effectiveness check {ref:CA-008}) and KPIs reported in management review (open CAPA count by age, average closure time, effectiveness pass rate).
